channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinians trek across rubble to return to their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes hold
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Even before the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas was fully in place Sunday, Palestinians in the war-battered Gaza Strip began to return to the remains of the homes they had evacuated during the 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Johnson vows there will be ‘hell to pay’ if Hamas violates ceasefire deal
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) vowed that there will be “hell to pay” if Hamas violates the ceasefire deal struck recently with Israel. Johnson joined NBC News’s “Meet the Press” on Sunday, saying he was hopeful about the truce announced last week.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump wants to visit China, strengthen relationship: Report
item.description: The president-elect has vowed to impose tariffs and other mechanisms against China upon his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Warm weather threatens Epiphany ice water plunges for Russia's Orthodox Christians
item.description: Thousands of Russians celebrating the Orthodox Christian feast day of Epiphany, where worshippers bathe in the icy waters of frozen lakes and rivers, have been forced to cancel their traditional ceremonies due to unusually warm winter temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taliban deputy tells leader there is no excuse for education bans on Afghan women and girls
item.description: A senior Taliban figure has urged the group's leader to scrap education bans on Afghan women and girls, saying there is no excuse for them, in a rare public rebuke of government policy.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An imprisoned Nobel laureate underscores human rights abuses in Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — The yellow name tag that Ales Bialiatski wears on his prison garb sets him apart from other inmates in Penal Colony No. 9 in eastern Belarus. It marks Bialiatski as a political prisoner to be singled out for harsh treatment.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korea's impeached president is arrested over martial law declaration and his supporters riot
item.description: South Korea’s impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, has been formally arrested, days after being apprehended at his presidential compound in Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Southeast Asia foreign ministers seek breakthrough in Myanmar conflict and South China Sea dispute
item.description: Southeast Asian foreign ministers met this year under the regional bloc’s new chair, Malaysia, seeking a breakthrough over Myanmar’s drawn-out civil war and territorial disputes in the South China Sea.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Ng, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mulvaney says 'hard to deny' Trump's influence on Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: Mick Mulvaney, former acting White House Chief of Staff during Trump’s first administration, said President-elect Trump should receive credit for Israel-Hamas ceasefire. “Look, obviously, both administrations, the outgoing Biden administration and the incoming administration, were involved in this.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ski lift accident leaves 30 injured at Spanish resort in the Pyrenees
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — At least 30 people have been injured in a ski lift accident at the Spanish resort of Astún, in the Pyrenees mountain range, emergency services for Spain's northern Aragon region said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Incoming British ambassador to US praises Trump in op-ed
item.description: The incoming British ambassador to the U.S. Peter Mandelson praised President-elect Trump in a new op-ed, predicting his forthcoming presidency will be one of the “most consequential” in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: UN announces new plan to counter the surge in antisemitism
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations announced new actions Friday to counter the surge in antisemitism, including encouraging governments to enforce laws against hate crimes and discrimination. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blessings for dogs? Bring them to Mexico City's cathedral and St. Anthony will do the rest
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Behaving at their best, a dozen dogs attended Mass at Mexico City’s cathedral Friday, waiting for their turn to be doused with holy water. The blessing of the animals is a long-time Catholic tradition celebrated on January 17.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 lawyers for the late Kremlin foe Alexei Navalny are convicted and sentenced to prison
item.description: PETUSHKI, Russia (AP) — Three lawyers who once represented the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny were convicted by a court Friday as part of the Kremlin’s crackdown on dissent that has reached levels unseen since Soviet times.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and Iran sign a partnership treaty to deepen their ties in the face of Western sanctions
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin and his Iranian counterpart, Masoud Pezeshkian, signed a broad cooperation pact Friday as their countries deepened their partnership in the face of stinging Western sanctions.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland is running to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Chrystia Freeland, whose abrupt resignation as finance minister last month forced Justin Trudeau's exit as prime minister, said she is running to be the next leader of the Liberal Party and prime minister of Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Xi discuss TikTok, trade in call before inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump said Friday he spoke with Chinese President Xi Jinping, with the two leaders discussing trade and the fate of the popular video-sharing app TikTok. "The call was a very good one for both China and the U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: South African miner describes horrors for those who spent months underground
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa. (AP) — Dozens of men sat and lay together in small caves, some so shallow they couldn’t stand up. The air was wet and stuffy, and wracking coughs echoed in the confined space. Bodies wrapped in fabric and twine were set aside in rows nearby.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of dead sea turtles are washing ashore on India's coast
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Over 400 endangered sea turtles have washed ashore on India's east coast near the city of Chennai in the last two weeks in an event not witnessed in over two decades. Olive ridley turtles travel thousands of miles for ideal nesting conditions on India’s coast.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia, Iran to seal partnership treaty days before Trump takes office
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian will meet in Moscow to sign a partnership pact as the two nations brace for President-elect Trump's return.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilian court denies Bolsonaro’s request to travel to Trump’s inauguration
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court has denied former President Jair Bolsonaro's request to have his passport temporarily restored in order to attend the inauguration of U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American college rape suspect is being extradited to the US
item.description: Ian Cleary, who is accused of sexually assaulting a Gettysburg College student at a party in 2013, is being sent back to the U.S., officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis injured as Vatican confirms 2nd fall in matter of weeks
item.description: Pope Francis fell for the second time in six weeks on Thursday, injuring his right arm and requiring a sling, the Vatican says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kirby 'confident' Americans in Gaza will be freed Sunday amid reports of Israel, Hamas deal holdup
item.description: The White House national security communications adviser John Kirby said he is "confident" a deal will be implemented on Sunday despite Israeli claims that Hamas has backtracked on deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says 'last-minute crisis' with Hamas holding up approval of Gaza truce and hostage deal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said Thursday his Cabinet won’t meet to approve the agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages until Hamas backs down, accusing the group of reneging on parts of the agreement in an atte
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu delays Gaza cease-fire vote, accusing Hamas of trying to back out of deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday accused Hamas of backing out of a cease-fire deal to release hostages and bring a pause to more than a year of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Families of hostages grateful but cautious about ceasefire deal
item.description: While some may soon reunite with their loved ones, others face the grim possibility of receiving confirmation of deaths during captivity.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Mozambique's Chapo sworn in as president after disputed election
item.description: Following months of protests against his disputed election victory, Daniel Chapo of Mozambique's Frelimo party has been sworn in as president at a sparsely attended ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Details need to be addressed in Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: When a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is finalized depends on the handling of specific implementation details
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Ceasefire in Gaza begins, Hamas releases hostage names to Israel
item.description: The ceasefire was delayed but will take effect Sunday after Hamas released the list of hostage names to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
